skill.name: industry-key-contact-radar-api-skill
skill.description: Discover key contacts across industries, roles, and social platforms via the BrowserAct API. Finds public profiles for founders, CEOs, and decision-makers with profile URLs, names, and company information.
skill.summary: API-based contact discovery tool for finding industry leaders and decision-makers across social platforms
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate API integration for contact discovery via BrowserAct. Static findings are false positives: network calls are to legitimate BrowserAct API endpoints, environment access is for required API key authentication, and detected external commands are documentation examples in SKILL.md. No malicious patterns confirmed after manual review.

content.user_title: Discover industry contacts via BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: Finding key contacts across industries and social platforms requires manual research and multiple tools. This skill uses the BrowserAct API to automatically discover and extract contact information including profile URLs, names, and company details.
actual_capabilities: Search for contacts by industry, job title, and social platform
Extract profile URLs, names, introductions, and company information
Support for multiple platforms including Facebook, LinkedIn, and GitHub
Polling-based task execution with status monitoring
Automatic error handling for authorization and rate limit issues
Return structured contact data suitable for CRM integration
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key with active subscription
Rate limits depend on the BrowserAct plan tier
Task completion time varies based on platform and search complexity

use_cases.title: Lead Generation for Sales Teams
use_cases.description: Find founders and decision-makers in target industries to build qualified prospect lists.
use_cases.target_user: Sales representatives and business development teams

use_cases.title: Talent Acquisition Research
use_cases.description: Discover growth leaders and key roles for recruiting outreach across professional networks.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Identify key personnel at competing organizations for market analysis and networking.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Contact Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find {number} {job_title} profiles in the {industry} industry on {site}.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple search for specific contacts by industry and role

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {job_title} at {company} across Facebook and LinkedIn. Get up to {number} results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-platform search for specific company contacts

prompt_templates.title: Industry Landscape Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Discover the top {number} marketing managers working in {industry} sector. Include their profiles from industry-relevant sites.
prompt_templates.scenario: Bulk industry-wide contact discovery for market research

prompt_templates.title: Custom Contact Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: I need contact information for {role} positions in the {vertical} vertical. Focus on {site} profiles and extract name, profile URL, and company affiliation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed custom extraction with specific parameters

output_examples.input: Find 5 founder profiles in the AI industry on LinkedIn
output_examples.output: Contact data returned with profile URLs, names, and company associations from LinkedIn search results

output_examples.input: Search for growth managers in SaaS companies on Facebook
output_examples.output: List of public profiles with names, profile links, and brief introductions

best_practices: Start with lower date limits and increase gradually to avoid rate limits
Use specific job titles instead of broad terms for more targeted results
Monitor task status logs during execution to identify issues early
anti_patterns: Do not run multiple concurrent searches without checking your plan limits
Avoid requesting extremely high record counts on first runs
Do not ignore authorization errors; verify your API key is valid

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why is it required?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a web automation service that handles browser-based scraping and data extraction. This skill uses their Industry Key Contact Radar template to reliably extract contact data from social platforms.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at browseract.com and navigate to the Integrations page to generate your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What social platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports any public website including Facebook, LinkedIn, GitHub, and other social or professional networking platforms.

faq.question: How long does a search typically take?
faq.answer: Task completion varies from 1-5 minutes depending on the platform, number of results requested, and current server load. The script polls until completion.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will output an authorization error. Do not retry; instead verify your API key is correct and active in your BrowserAct account.

faq.question: Can I run multiple searches at once?
faq.answer: This depends on your BrowserAct plan tier. If you exceed concurrent task limits, the API will return an error. Upgrade your plan for higher limits.
